If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.51-3 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @abbagail **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774999194253479) ## Action Performed: 1. From Account A, create a reimbursable expense/report in a personal workspace 2. Submit the report to User B 3. Log in as User B in New Expensify 4. On Home, click Pay shortcut 5. Observe the report shows Done status with a “Pay” button 6. Click Pay > Mark as Paid > Nothing happens 7. Open the report : Expense is not visible, No Pay option available 8. Switch to Classic Expensify 9. Check Home and report ## Expected Result: Report should: Appear consistently as payable across platforms Allow Mark as Paid action to complete successfully Display expense details within the report ## Actual Result: ND Home shows Pay option, but action does nothing Inside report: Expense is not visible No Pay option available Classic: Report does not appear as needing payment No payment action available ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c039d85-9285-433f-bb12-1aa4b0a7110d [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.51-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-14595/folder/13176706/44011822/1295551573 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+ccardfeed@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Windows 11 / Chrome **App Component:** Chat Report View ## Action Performed: Preconditions: log out if already logged in, then log in to applausetester+ccardfeed@applause.expensifail.com account Steps: 1. Navigate to Reports tab 2. Click on "Unapproved card" option in "Monthly accrual" section 3. Note that the card Feed pill is auto selected to American Express 4. Click on the card transaction, note that the transaction doesn't display correctly 5. Click on the Feed pill 6. Unselect then select the "American Express" option again, then click "apply" button 7. Click the card transaction expense again, note that the transaction now display correctly 8. Click "arrow" on the card transaction to open the expense details ## Expected Result: - in step 4, the card transaction should be displayed correctly under each column (e.g receipt, type, date, merchant, and total) without unselect and select the Feed pill - in step 8, the expense details should be opened when user click "arrow" on the displayed card transaction ## Actual Result: - in step 4, the card transaction doesn't display correctly - in step 8, clicking "arrow" on the card transaction doesn't lead to the expense details ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1337f0a6-22d7-4ac6-92dd-6b56e8cfedb8 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Create new expensifail account 2. Have a default workspace with distance rate enabled 3. Have odometer beta Steps: 1. Navigate to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Press the global create button > Create expense > Manual 3. Enter any amount and go next 4. Observe it goes to the confirm details page 5. Go back and switch to Scan 6. Add an image and go next 7. Observe it goes to the confirm details page 8. Close the RHP 9. Press the global create button > Track distance > Map 10. Enter any waypoints and go next 11. Observe it goes to the confirm details page 12. Go back and switch to Manual 13. Enter any amount and go next 14. Observe it goes to the confirm details page 15. Go back and switch to Odometer 16. Enter any amount and go next 17. Observe it goes to the confirm details page ## Expected Result: On steps 4, 7, 11, 14, 17 - Participant page should open ## Actual Result: On steps 4, 7, 11, 14, 17 - Confirm details page opens instead of participant page ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/64bbc2a7-416f-4015-a4cb-6fd362fcfd6e [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace chat. 3. Create two reports with an expense in each report. 4. Go to Reports > Reports. 5. Go offline. 6. Open any report from Step 3. 7. Click More > Merge. 8. Select the other expense from Step 3 > Continue. 9. Select all the second options for each section. 10. Click Continue > Merge expenses. ## Expected Result: Report RHP will open with expense details after merging expenses offline. ## Actual Result: Report RHP is empty after merging expenses offline. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/59dcaa20-5c72-40ef-b9fa-c2596361ce76 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Enable Hermes V1

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Proposal: Enable Hermes V1 ### Background Hermes V1 is the next evolution of the Hermes JS engine that powers our iOS and Android apps, providing performance improvements such as just-in-time (JIT) compilation and JS-to-native compilation (formerly referred to as "Static Hermes"). Meta tested it against several popular JS benchmarks and saw significant performance improvements across the board. They also benchmarked it in the Expensify app (in October, 2025) and found that it reduced: - total TTI by 2.7% - bundle time by 8.9% - content TTI by 7.6% (Screenshots taken from the [React Conf 2025 React Native Keynote](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NiYwlvXsBKw)) ### Problem When users open the iOS or Android apps, if the P90 case takes >5s (current threshold for ManualAppStartup metric), then they are less likely to perceive the app as responsive, which reduces the likelihood that they will continue to use it. ### Solution Enable Hermes V1 - reduce total TTI by ~2.7%. This should reduce our P90 by ~20-30ms on iOS and ~50-60ms on Android, and generally make JS runtime performance measurably better across the board.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @mananjadhav

title: [$250] Crash when attendee has undefined email in getPersonalDetailByEmail

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem Expense attendees can have `undefined` for their `email` field, which causes New Expensify to crash with "Uh-oh, something went wrong!" when rendering the affected report. The crash occurs because `getPersonalDetailByEmail()` in `src/libs/PersonalDetailsUtils.ts` calls `email.toLowerCase()` without guarding against `undefined`. When `useReportPreviewSenderID.ts` iterates over transaction attendees and calls `getPersonalDetailByEmail(att.email)` with an attendee whose email is `undefined`, the app throws a `TypeError`. **Related internal issue:** Expensify/Expensify#618370 ## Crash Chain 1. A transaction has an attendee object with `email: undefined` in `transaction.comment.attendees` 2. `useReportPreviewSenderID.ts:72-77` iterates over attendees and calls `getPersonalDetailByEmail(att.email)` 3. `getPersonalDetailByEmail` at `PersonalDetailsUtils.ts:143` executes `email.toLowerCase()` on `undefined` 4. `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toLowerCase')` → crash screen ## Why This Happens The `Attendee` TypeScript type (at `src/types/onyx/IOU.ts`) declares `email: string` (required), but Onyx data loaded from persistence/backend is not validated at runtime. Legacy data (e.g., attendees created through OldDot where only `displayName` was required) can have no `email` key at all, resulting in `undefined` at runtime. ## Affected Code Locations | File | Line | Risk | |------|------|------| | `src/libs/PersonalDetailsUtils.ts` | 143-144 | **Crash origin** — `getPersonalDetailByEmail` calls `.toLowerCase()` on `undefined` | | `src/components/ReportActionAvatars/useReportPreviewSenderID.ts` | 72-77 | **High** — crash site calling `getPersonalDetailByEmail(att.email)` | | `src/pages/iou/request/MoneyRequestAttendeeSelector.tsx` | 91 | **High** — same unguarded `getPersonalDetailByEmail(attendee.email)` pattern | | `src/libs/OptionsListUtils/index.ts` | 2864 | **High** — processes `recentAttendees` from Onyx with same pattern | ## Recommended Fix Add a defensive guard in `getPersonalDetailByEmail` to handle `undefined`/empty email: ```typescript function getPersonalDetailByEmail(email: string): PersonalDetails | undefined { if (!email) { return undefined; } return emailToPersonalDetailsCache[email.toLowerCase()]; } ``` This single change protects all 50+ callers across the codebase, including the 3 other vulnerable attendee-related call sites listed above. Several other callers already use `?? ''` to guard their input, but fixing it centrally is safer than patching each call site individually.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @jayeshmangwani
